level_signals: 7+ years of program management, business operations, or management consulting experience
Accountable for building and driving large-scale transformational initiatives end to end
Expected to create strong business cases and drive influence across cross-functional teams

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7+ years of program management, business operations, or management consulting experience, preferably in a GTM or Sales Operations organization
must_haves.qualifier: preferably in GTM or Sales Operations

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Demonstrated organizational, project management, and execution skills with ability to own cross-functional projects
An ability to drive sales territory planning and align strategies across all segments to ensure seamless execution
Strong track record as a strategic problem solver and skilled operator with the demonstrated ability to successfully deploy new initiatives end to end
A high degree of comfort working in a high-growth and fast-paced environment
An ability to seamlessly transition from the high level to very detailed work, and a hands-on approach
A detail-oriented individual with business acumen, and the ability to influence and communicate across all levels of the organization

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Knowledge of Customer Relationship Management (CRM) systems
Data analytics (Google Sheets or Excel data manipulation, SQL)

scope.surface: GTM Planning — sales planning cycle, territory planning, cross-functional process transformation
primary_metrics_inferred: On-time delivery of large-scale transformational programs
core_responsibilities: Lead and manage day-to-day operations of large-scale transformational projects
Develop detailed project plans, set milestones, and ensure program integrity
Design solutions for process improvements and tool development
Coordinate with key stakeholders and leadership to align complex programs and secure buy-in
Be accountable for building and driving large-scale transformational initiatives end to end
Create strong business cases and drive influence to gain cross-functional support
Identify, assess, and mitigate risks that impact program delivery
Create roadmaps, project plans, change management plans, and reporting methodologies
Manage change management activities across stakeholder engagement
Influence and adopt AI-based tools to make planning processes more efficient

green_flags: Clear team charter with named cross-functional partners (Finance, Legal, Systems, Product, Sales Leadership)
JD explicitly states preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement — signals openness in hiring
Emphasis on AI-based tool adoption signals a forward-looking team
Role owns high-visibility, large-scale transformation programs — meaningful scope
red_flags: No location, salary, or remote/hybrid policy disclosed anywhere in the JD
No visa sponsorship or work authorization details provided
GTM/Sales Operations preference in a minimum requirement creates ambiguity about how strictly it is enforced
Broad scope with no team size information makes it hard to assess support structure

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 7+ years requirement and scope of responsibilities)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false; no people management language in JD)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
compensation.equity_mentioned (inferred as false; not mentioned in JD)
location.type (set to 'unclear'; no location details in JD body)
ambiguities: The minimum requirement states '7+ years … preferably in a GTM or Sales Operations organization' — 'preferably' suggests this context qualifier may not be strictly enforced, but the requirement is listed under minimum requirements, not preferred qualifications.
No location or office policy is specified anywhere in the JD; location.type set to 'unclear'.
